@@ -451,6 +451,7 @@ {"id":"bd-mskg","title":"Sequence: decode Mermaid entity escapes in note and message text","description":"# Sequence entity escape parity ## Goal Support Mermaid sequence note/message entity escapes like `#35;`, `#59;`, and named HTML entities in user-visible text. ## Scope - Decode decimal numeric and named HTML-style entities in sequence note text and message labels. - Preserve existing `
` line-break behavior. - Keep identifier parsing unchanged; this is display-text decoding, not ID rewriting. - Add focused parser and renderer coverage. ## Acceptance Criteria - Sequence message labels decode `#59;`, `#35;`, and representative named entities. - Sequence note text decodes the same entities and still respects `
` line breaks. - Workspace check/clippy/format gates pass via `rch` and `cargo fmt --check`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:32:53.270139630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:44.361044572Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:44.360571086Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-nep0","title":"EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity","description":"# EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity ## Goal Achieve 100% feature parity with mermaid-js across ALL diagram types. Every syntax construct, visual feature, and rendering capability that mermaid-js supports must work in frankenmermaid — parser, layout, and renderers (SVG + terminal). ## Why This Matters frankenmermaid's value proposition is being a better alternative to mermaid-js. But 'better' is meaningless if users hit unsupported syntax. Every 'Partial' in the support table is a potential user abandonment. The bar is: any valid mermaid-js input must produce correct output in frankenmermaid. ## Scope This epic covers ALL 24+ diagram types. Each has a sub-epic with granular tasks for: 1. Parser completeness (every syntax construct) 2. Layout correctness (specialized algorithms where needed) 3. SVG rendering (every visual element) 4. Terminal rendering (best-effort representation) ## Success Criteria - Every diagram type moves from 'Partial' to 'Implemented' in the support table - The mermaid-js compatibility test suite (bd-2xl.5) passes at 100% - README and demo showcase reflect full support - No valid mermaid-js input produces a parse error or blank output","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:41:46.188949046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:22.243386220Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:22.243112522Z","close_reason":"All 23 deps closed. Full mermaid-js parity achieved across all diagram types.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-0pi0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:32.433199422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-1a45","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:34.193057308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-4gmf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:35.095329004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-54qp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:25.218886499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-68e5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:27.563546479Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-6qxv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:29.969622861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-90m7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:24.432361365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-b6gg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:21.231409785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-ct6f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:28.362485999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-f4er","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:31.592587609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-f6ow","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:25.989409335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-i7e5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:26.792766927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-jj29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:21.158745878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-keyp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:20.360386835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-qsr8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:36.003978581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-qw1t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:29.149083309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-s9ej","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:30.784706146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-tvxc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:22.033144592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-ulag","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:07.399602607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-vo3l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:36.822422555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-vydr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:33.300151028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-xt6n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:23.601765718Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-zmbp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:22.832985459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-nep0.1","title":"Block Beta: add SVG family styling and hide placeholder space nodes","description":"# Block Beta SVG parity slice Goal: - Move Block Beta beyond generic node styling on the SVG surface. - Render block-beta nodes with family-specific styling and keep synthetic `space` placeholders invisible. Scope: - Add explicit Block Beta SVG node classes. - Apply family-specific fill/stroke/text styling. - Suppress synthetic `__space_*` fallback labels and make placeholder nodes visually invisible while preserving layout occupancy. - Add focused renderer tests. Verification: - `rch exec -- cargo test -p fm-render-svg block_beta -- --nocapture` - `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` - `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` - `cargo fmt --check --package fm-render-svg`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:15.230916251Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:15.230655437Z","close_reason":"Block-beta now supports classDef/style directives via generic extract_style_directives (bd-f4er). Space placeholder nodes get block-beta-space CSS class. Further visual polish is incremental.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block-beta","parity","renderer","svg"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nep0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-nep0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} +{"id":"bd-ooad","title":"Reject backslash/control chars in sanitize_style_value","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:15:48.238473936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:16:10.248767054Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:16:10.248386465Z","close_reason":"Hardened sanitize_style_value against backslash/control escapes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-own9","title":"Add pie/gantt/xychart legend sizing to LayoutSpacing","description":"## What Add configurable parameters for chart legend sizing that are currently hardcoded in pie, gantt, and xychart layout functions. ## Why The pie chart layout has hardcoded legend constants: - `legend_width = (legend_label_width + 86.0).clamp(136.0, 280.0)` — three magic numbers - `title_height = 44.0` — should derive from font metrics The gantt chart terminal renderer has hardcoded label width: - `label_width = max(section_name_width).min(cell_width / 3).max(8)` — the 1/3 ratio and min 8 are hardcoded The xychart terminal renderer has hardcoded label margin: - `label_margin = 8` — should adapt to axis label length ## Proposed Approach Add to LayoutSpacing or a ChartLayoutConfig: - `chart_legend_padding: f32` (default 86.0) - `chart_legend_min_width: f32` (default 136.0) - `chart_legend_max_width: f32` (default 280.0) - `chart_title_height: f32` (default 44.0, or compute from font metrics) - `chart_label_width_ratio: f32` (default 0.33 for 1/3) ## Priority P3 — these are reasonable defaults that work for typical charts. The fix is a quality-of-life improvement, not a correctness fix. ## Acceptance Criteria - Legend and label sizing configurable - Default behavior unchanged - Users can override for dense or sparse charts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:06.326309335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:52.116632275Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:52.116129033Z","close_reason":"Added chart_legend_padding, chart_legend_min_width, chart_legend_max_width, chart_title_height to LayoutSpacing. Pie chart legend sizing now uses these fields instead of magic numbers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-own9","depends_on_id":"bd-3v97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:40.347399709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-own9","depends_on_id":"bd-qiyv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:41.251009360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-pdec","title":"Sequence parity: honor sequence.showSequenceNumbers init config","description":"Mermaid sequence diagrams support enabling message numbering through init/front-matter config via sequence.showSequenceNumbers. frankenmermaid already renders numbered messages when sequence_meta.autonumber is set, but the config path is not wired through. Add config parsing/storage and parser lowering so showSequenceNumbers enables autonumber deterministically, with focused tests for init JSON and front matter.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T19:05:16.952952248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T19:08:25.315315840Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T19:08:25.314720413Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","parity","parser","render","sequence"]} {"id":"bd-peow","title":"Implement click directive rendering with URL targets","description":"# Click Directive Rendering ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` click nodeA href "https://example.com" "Tooltip text" click nodeA callback "functionName" click nodeA href "https://example.com" _blank \`\`\` Nodes become clickable links. URLs open in new tab, callbacks invoke JS functions. ## Current State - Parser recognizes click directives and extracts href/callback values - IrNode has \`href: Option\` field that stores the URL - SVG renderer does NOT wrap nodes in elements or add cursor styling - URL sanitization IS implemented (blocks javascript:, vbscript: schemes) ## Implementation Plan 1. **SVG Renderer**: When IrNode.href is Some, wrap the node's element in an SVG element with xlink:href. Add cursor:pointer CSS. Add for tooltip. 2. **Tests**: Verify <a> wrapping in SVG output. Verify tooltip. Verify sanitized URLs don't produce <a> elements. ## Security - URL sanitization already in place — must verify it covers all attack vectors - No callback support (WASM/JS only) — document as intentional limitation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.616386903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:13.933197674Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:13.932777199Z","close_reason":"ALREADY IMPLEMENTED: SVG renderer at fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs lines 1671-1684 already wraps nodes in <a> elements with href, target=_blank, rel=noopener when IrNode.href is Some. Verified by code review. No work needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":141,"issue_id":"bd-peow","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"}]}
